content.problemStatement:
You have an array AAA of NNN integers, and an integer MMM such that 0‚â§Ai<M0 \le A_i < M0‚â§Ai‚Äã<M.

You can do the following operation as many times as you want:


Set Ai=(Ai+1)mod‚Äâ‚ÄâMA_i = (A_i + 1) \mod MAi‚Äã=(Ai‚Äã+1)modM for **all indices** iii (1‚â§i‚â§N1 \le i \le N1‚â§i‚â§N).
Find the minimum possible sum of the array AAA after doing the above operation some number of times.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of multiple lines of input.

The first line of each test case contains 222 integers - NNN and MMM, the size of the array and the modulo.
The second line contains NNN integers - A1,A2,‚Ä¶,ANA_1, A_2, \ldots, A_NA1‚Äã,A2‚Äã,‚Ä¶,AN‚Äã.




Output Format
For each test case, output on a new line the minimum possible sum after some operations.
constraints: The sum of NNN and the sum of MMM both do not exceed 2‚ãÖ1052 \cdot 10^52‚ãÖ105 over all test cases.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test Case 1 : We do not perform any operation.
Test Case 2 : We perform the operation 999999999 times, and that changes our array to [0,0][0, 0][0,0] which has a sum of 000.
